# programs.macos-terminal

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `programs.macos-terminal`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `programs.macos-terminal.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable macOS Terminal. |
| `programs.macos-terminal.preferences` | `submodule` | Options controlling how Terminal.app preferences are applied and managed. |
| `programs.macos-terminal.preferences.importSettings` | `boolean` | Whether to import the generated plist into the `com.apple.Terminal` preferences domain during activation. |
| `programs.macos-terminal.preferences.writeFile` | `boolean` | Whether to write the generated plist into the `~/Library/Preferences/com.apple.Terminal.plist` file during activation. This is primarily useful for inspection and testing purposes. |
| `programs.macos-terminal.profiles` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Configuration settings for profiles within the Terminal.app application. Each attribute name is used as the name of the profile, and the value defines the plist-compatible settings for that profile. |
| `programs.macos-terminal.profiles.<name>.settings` | `attribute set of (Property list (plist) value)` | Raw plist-compatible settings for profiles within the Terminal.app application. This attribute set is intended for simple settings that have a well defined mapping to plist properties. Properties that are more obscure and require serialization as archived Cocoa objects, for example, are unsupported here - they may require dedicated module options in future. Attribute names should reflect the name of plist properties understood by the Terminal.app application. Unknown attributes will still be serialized, but may remain unrecognised - and thus unhonored - by the Terminal.app application. |
